heading: Index Definition Examples
body:
The following examples for the `sample_mflix.movies` collection create
index definitions that use [dynamic mappings](static-dynamic-mappings). These examples use the `synonymous_terms`
synonym source collection described in the [Synonym Source Collection Example](sample-synonyms-coll)
section to enable synonym mappings.

This example performs the following actions:

- Configures an index with a single text field and a single
  synonym mapping definition that uses the mapping configured in
  the `synonymous_terms` collection.

- Analyzes the `plot` field with the `lucene.english`
  analyzer.

- Enables synonyms from the `synonymous_terms` collection for queries
  on fields analyzed with the `lucene.english` analyzer.

You can use the Atlas Administration API to configure the index.
Replace the following values in the sample `curl` command:

- Replace \{`ACCESS-TOKEN`} with your Service Account access token.
  To learn how to generate an access token, see [Make an API (Application Programming Interface) Request](example-api-request).

- Replace \{`groupId`} with the project ID of the project where
  you want to create the MongoDB Search index.

- Replace \{`clusterName`} with the name of the cluster where
  you want to create the MongoDB Search index.

```shell
curl --header "Authorization: Bearer ${ACCESS_TOKEN}" \
    --header "Accept: application/vnd.atlas.2025-03-12+json" \
    --header "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -X POST "https://cloud.mongodb.com/api/atlas/v2/groups/{groupId}/clusters/{clusterName}/search/indexes" \
    -d '{ "collectionName": "movies",
        "database": "sample_mflix",
        "name": "default",
        "type": "search",
        "definition": {
            "mappings": {
              "dynamic": false,
              "fields": {
                "plot": {
                  "type": "string",
                  "analyzer": "lucene.english"
                }
              }
            },
            "synonyms": [
              {
                "analyzer": "lucene.english",
                "name": "my_synonyms",
                "source": {
                  "collection": "synonymous_terms"
                }
              }
            ]
        }
    }'
```

This example performs the following actions:

- Configures an index for all document fields and a
  single synonym mapping definition that uses the mapping
  configured in the `synonymous_terms` collection.

- Uses the default analyzer, `lucene.standard`, to analyze all
  the fields.

- Enables synonyms from the `synonymous_terms` collection for queries on
  fields analyzed with the `lucene.standard` analyzer.

You can use the Atlas Administration API to configure the index.
Replace the following values in the sample `curl` command:

- Replace \{`ACCESS-TOKEN`} with your Service Account access token.
  To learn how to generate an access token, see [Make an API (Application Programming Interface) Request](example-api-request).

- Replace \{`groupId`} with the project ID of the project where
  you want to create the MongoDB Search index.

- Replace \{`clusterName`} with the name of the cluster where
  you want to create the MongoDB Search index.

```shell
curl --header "Authorization: Bearer ${ACCESS_TOKEN}" \
    --header "Accept: application/vnd.atlas.2025-03-12+json" \
    --header "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -X POST "https://cloud.mongodb.com/api/atlas/v2/groups/{groupId}/clusters/{clusterName}/search/indexes" \
    -d '{ "collectionName": "movies",
        "database": "sample_mflix",
        "name": "default",
        "type": "search",
        "definition": {
            "mappings": {
              "dynamic": true
            },
            "synonyms": [
              {
                "analyzer": "lucene.standard",
                "name": "my_synonyms",
                "source": {
                  "collection": "synonymous_terms"
                }
              }
            ]
        }
    }'
```

To learn how to add a collection that configures words as synonyms to your cluster, and use this collection to
create a MongoDB Search index and run MongoDB Search queries using synonyms, see [How to Use Synonyms with MongoDB Search](synonyms-tutorial).

To see examples of `text` queries that use synonyms, see [Synonyms Examples](text-ref-synonyms-eg) in the [text](text-ref) reference page.

To see an example `phrase` queries that uses synonyms, see [Synonyms Example](fts-phrase-synonyms-example) in the [Phrase](phrase-ref) reference page.

The following examples for the `sample_mflix.movies` collection create
index definitions that use [dynamic mappings](static-dynamic-mappings). These examples use the `synonymous_terms`
synonym source collection described in the [Synonym Source Collection Example](sample-synonyms-coll)
section to enable synonym mappings.

This example performs the following actions:

- Configures an index with a single text field and a single
  synonym mapping definition that uses the mapping configured in
  the `synonymous_terms` collection.

- Analyzes the `plot` field with the `lucene.english`
  analyzer.

- Enables synonyms from the `synonymous_terms` collection for queries
  on fields analyzed with the `lucene.english` analyzer.
